title: Regex sanitizer for all sinks
description: A single sanitizer is reused across SQL, HTML, shell, URL, or path contexts.
body: Failure mode: code applies a regex or string replacement and treats the output as safe for multiple interpreters. The same payload can be harmless in one context and executable in another, especially after decoding or concatenation. Detection signals include sanitize() helpers with broad names, raw query fragments, string-built commands, or tests that do not render or execute the final sink. Remediation is sink-specific structured APIs plus malicious payload tests. Human gate is required for dynamic code or command sinks. Use when a proposed approach skips trust boundaries, authorization decisions, dangerous sinks, secrets, and dependency execution or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in negative security tests, server-side checks, sink payload tests, and secret scans. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause client-only controls, injection, credential leakage, permissive fallback, and unsafe dependency changes.
